Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low CO₂, high chloride, and significant proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function with possible metabolic acidosis. Marked protein, glucose, and ketones in urine indicate possible diabetic nephropathy or acute kidney injury with glycosuria and ketonuria. Elevated ALT suggests mild hepatic involvement. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity, supporting renal pathology. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and assessment for diabetes and renal impairment.
